summary: Full backend + frontend testing of Contractor Check-In MVP. 21/21 pytest backend tests pass; all frontend flows pass (public check-in with GDPR modal, validation, geolocation POST, admin login+JWT, protected route redirect, wrong-password error, Check-Ins tab table+live map, Jobs CRUD, Site Content update+persist, logout). One user-facing bug found: navigator.clipboard.writeText in JobsTab.copyLink is not wrapped in try/catch; when the browser denies clipboard permission, the unhandled promise rejection triggers React's uncaught runtime error overlay in dev and no user-friendly toast is shown.

ui_bugs.issue: navigator.clipboard.writeText() returns a Promise; when it rejects (e.g. permission denied or non-HTTPS context) the error is unhandled -> React shows 'Uncaught runtime errors: Failed to execute writeText on Clipboard: Write permission denied' overlay, blocking further clicks. No error toast. Should be wrapped in try/catch (or .catch) with a graceful toast + fallback (e.g. prompt() or document.execCommand fallback).

issues: Radix DialogContent warning: Missing `Description` or `aria-describedby` on job-dialog and privacy-modal – non-blocking a11y warning but should add DialogDescription for screen readers.

action_items: Wrap navigator.clipboard.writeText in try/catch in AdminDashboard.copyLink and show a toast on failure (also handle Promise rejection).
Optional a11y: add <DialogDescription> to PrivacyModal (already has one) and JobDialog to remove Radix warning.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: response_model_by_alias=False is set correctly on jobs/checkins routes so `id` (not `_id`) is returned – verified in tests.
server.py: startup seed correctly updates admin password when env changes (verify_password check + update) – matches playbook.
server.py: bcrypt hash format uses bcrypt.hashpw with gensalt() -> $2b$ prefix (correct).
server.py: CheckInInput uses EmailStr validation; job_id validated with ObjectId.is_valid.

AdminDashboard.js is 350+ lines with three sub-components (CheckInsTab, JobsTab, SettingsTab) plus JobDialog. Consider splitting into separate files under /components/admin/ for maintainability, but not blocking.
AdminDashboard copyLink lacks error handling for clipboard API (see ui_bugs).
CheckInPage.js: validate() dereferences `job.custom_fields` unconditionally after job-load; safe because it's only called inside handleShareClick after job is loaded.
AuthContext.js: token stored in localStorage 'cc_token' as spec; refresh via /auth/me on mount is correct.
No brute-force lockout implemented on /api/auth/login. If required by playbook (5 fails), add rate limiting. Not in the review request feature list but flagging.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on core flows after re-run; initial run hit clipboard-error overlay that blocked delete/settings/logout steps

seed_data_creation: Backend seeds default job 'TechSpider Site — Downtown Tower' and admin user on startup. Frontend test created one Jane Contractor check-in on the seeded job (kept, harmless). All AUTOTEST_ jobs cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from env, admin creds hardcoded from test_credentials.md). Frontend flows use data-testids listed in review_request. Geolocation must be granted via context.grant_permissions + set_geolocation({lat:40.7128,lng:-74.006}). The clipboard bug in copyLink triggers React error overlay in dev builds and interferes with subsequent Playwright clicks – skip or fix before re-testing that flow.
rca of the issue: copyLink error overlay RCA: navigator.clipboard.writeText() returns a Promise. In JobsTab.copyLink it is called without await/catch. When the browser denies clipboard write (headless Chromium without clipboard-write permission, or non-focused window) the promise rejects unhandled. React 18 dev overlay captures the unhandled rejection and renders a full-screen error card intercepting pointer events. Mitigation: `try { await navigator.clipboard.writeText(url); toast.success(...);} catch { toast.error('Copy failed – link: ' + url); }`.
